data.title: Amazon warns 300 million users of dangerous holiday scams
data.description:
Amazon has issued an urgent alert to its massive user base of more than 300 million active customers, cautioning them about a significant uptick in sophisticated scams targeting holiday shoppers. As Black Friday deals and Christmas shopping reach their peak, cybercriminals are working overtime to exploit unsuspecting consumers through increasingly clever impersonation tactics and phishing schemes.

The e-commerce behemoth sent out a comprehensive warning email detailing how hackers are attempting to steal personal information, financial data and account credentials by masquerading as legitimate Amazon representatives. These deceptive tactics often appear through browser notifications or third-party platforms, tricking shoppers into voluntarily surrendering their login information without realizing they’ve fallen victim to a scam.


Five common scams targeting Amazon shoppers

Amazon‘s warning email outlined specific tactics that cybercriminals are employing during the holiday rush. Understanding these methods can help shoppers protect themselves from falling prey to these schemes.

The first scam involves fake delivery notifications or account alerts that appear authentic but originate from fraudulent sources. These messages create a sense of urgency, prompting users to click on malicious links or provide sensitive information.


Second, hackers are creating deceptive third-party advertisements on social media platforms, promising unbelievable deals that seem too good to pass up. These ads redirect shoppers to fake websites designed to harvest their personal data.

The third tactic includes messages from unofficial communication channels requesting account information or payment details. These messages often mimic Amazon’s branding and tone, making them difficult to distinguish from legitimate correspondence.

Fourth, suspicious links embedded in emails, text messages or social media posts serve as gateways to phishing websites. Once clicked, these links can install malware or direct users to counterfeit pages designed to capture login credentials.

The fifth scam involves unsolicited technical support calls from individuals claiming to represent Amazon’s customer service team. These callers use social engineering techniques to extract sensitive information from unsuspecting victims.

Why these scams are particularly effective now

While these fraudulent tactics aren’t entirely new to the digital landscape, Amazon emphasizes that they continue evolving and becoming more sophisticated. The holiday shopping season creates perfect conditions for these scams to thrive, as consumers are making more online purchases, tracking multiple deliveries and dealing with higher transaction volumes than usual.

The combination of shopping urgency, attractive deals and increased online activity makes people more susceptible to falling for these schemes. Cybercriminals exploit the chaos and excitement surrounding major shopping events like Black Friday and Cyber Monday, knowing that shoppers may be less cautious when hunting for bargains.

How customers can protect themselves

Amazon provided several practical recommendations for keeping personal information and financial details secure during the holiday shopping season. The company stressed that shoppers should exclusively use the official Amazon website or mobile application for all customer service needs, including tracking deliveries, modifying account settings or requesting refunds.

Setting up two-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security to accounts, requiring users to verify their identity through a second method beyond just a password. This simple step significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access.

Amazon also highlighted passkeys as a modern security alternative to traditional passwords. This technology uses biometric verification methods such as facial recognition or fingerprint scanning, or relies on a personal identification number for account access. Passkeys offer enhanced security while providing a more convenient user experience.

The company reiterated an important policy that genuine Amazon representatives will never request payment information or account credentials through phone calls or emails. Any communication asking for these details should be treated as suspicious and reported immediately.

Staying vigilant during peak shopping season

As online retail activity reaches its annual zenith, maintaining heightened awareness becomes essential for protecting personal information and financial assets. The convenience of e-commerce comes with inherent risks, particularly during periods when cybercriminal activity intensifies.

Shoppers can safeguard themselves by carefully examining all communications claiming to be from Amazon, verifying sender information and avoiding clicking on links in unsolicited messages. When in doubt, accessing Amazon directly through the official website or app rather than following links in emails or text messages provides the safest route.

Taking these precautions helps ensure that the holiday shopping experience remains enjoyable and secure, allowing millions of customers to take advantage of seasonal deals without falling victim to sophisticated scams designed to steal their hard-earned money or compromise their personal information.

This article is based on reporting from Mandatory and Forbes.

data.title: OpenAI confirms screenless AI gadget is complete
data.description:
OpenAI has officially entered the AI hardware arena with confirmation that its debut device prototype is complete. CEO Sam Altman made the announcement during an appearance at Emerson Collective’s 2025 Demo Day, joined by legendary Apple design chief Jony Ive and Emerson Collective founder Laurene Powell Jobs. The milestone marks a significant shift for the company best known for ChatGPT, as it ventures beyond software to create physical products powered by its advanced AI models.

The device represents a departure from traditional smartphones and tablets, embracing what Altman and Ive call ambient intelligence. Rather than demanding constant attention through screens and notifications, this prototype operates discreetly in the background, observing and learning from its surroundings before responding when truly needed.


A different kind of smart device

OpenAI’s hardware takes a bold approach by eliminating screens entirely. Instead, the device relies on an array of cameras and microphones to perceive its environment, building spatial awareness that allows it to understand context and adapt to individual users over time. This sensor-based design prioritizes observation over interaction, filtering out digital clutter rather than adding to it.

Ive’s influence is evident throughout the prototype’s design philosophy. Altman described the device as simple, beautiful and playful, suggesting a tactile experience that feels natural and inviting. The minimalist aesthetic aligns with Ive’s celebrated work at Apple, where he championed products that forge emotional connections with users through thoughtful design and intuitive functionality.


Portable intelligence that fits your lifestyle

The prototype is designed for portability, small enough to slip into a pocket or sit comfortably on a desk. While details remain limited, reports suggest the device may incorporate a small projector capable of displaying information on nearby surfaces, eliminating the need for a built-in screen while still providing visual output when necessary.

Connectivity appears central to the device’s capabilities. By linking to smartphones, laptops and other personal devices, the hardware can maintain a continuous memory of user activities and preferences. This persistent context-awareness sets it apart from existing smart assistants, enabling the device to anticipate needs and surface only relevant information while filtering out everything else.

Testing multiple possibilities

OpenAI’s development team explored various form factors during the prototype phase, testing desktop, mobile, wearable and portable configurations before landing on the current design. This extensive experimentation suggests a commitment to finding the right balance between functionality and user experience, rather than rushing to market with an untested concept.

Building the infrastructure to support it

OpenAI’s hardware ambitions extend beyond consumer devices. The company recently partnered with manufacturing giant Foxconn to develop next-generation AI data center infrastructure in the United States. While primarily focused on building custom server racks, cooling systems and networking equipment, the collaboration signals OpenAI’s desire to control the entire technology stack supporting its AI systems.

This vertical integration strategy could prove crucial as OpenAI scales its operations and deploys increasingly sophisticated AI models. By designing infrastructure specifically optimized for its workloads, the company aims to improve efficiency and maintain competitive advantages as AI systems grow more complex and demanding.

Taking on tech giants

OpenAI’s entry into consumer hardware puts it in direct competition with established players like Amazon, Google and Meta, all of whom have invested heavily in AI-powered devices with mixed results. Smart speakers and virtual assistants have achieved widespread adoption, but truly innovative AI hardware remains elusive. Many existing products still rely on screen-based interactions and struggle to deliver the seamless, contextual intelligence that users expect from advanced AI.

The screenless approach championed by OpenAI and Ive could represent the breakthrough the industry needs. By rethinking how humans interact with AI-powered devices, the prototype may offer a glimpse of a future where technology integrates more naturally into daily life without demanding constant attention.

While no release date has been announced, the completion of a working prototype suggests OpenAI is moving forward with its hardware plans. The collaboration between Altman’s AI expertise and Ive’s design mastery has produced a device that could reshape expectations for how artificial intelligence fits into our lives.

Story credit: TECHSPOT

data.title: Nintendo Switch games get rare discounts for Black Friday
data.description:
Nintendo has made some of the most iconic video games of all time, but building a library of standouts can quickly become expensive, especially given that many titles don’t often go on sale at significant discounts. The good news is that Nintendo is already running a holiday sale in the run up to Black Friday, allowing shoppers to save on Nintendo Switch games, amiibo and accessories through November 28th.

The biggest news is that Nintendo and select retailers are offering some of the Switch’s best games for as little as $29.99, which amounts to a healthy $30 discount. In some cases, these games are at their lowest price ever. These titles aren’t expected to be this heavily discounted again until next year’s spring promotions roll around.


Games hitting the $30 price point include Super Mario Odyssey, which remains one of the crowning achievements of the original Switch thanks to its tight platforming and ingenious level design. Princess Peach Showtime marks the first Nintendo game to star the Mushroom Kingdom royal in nearly 20 years. Donkey Kong Country Returns HD is the definitive version of the tough platformer that initially launched on the Wii in 2016.

Zelda and other top titles included

The Legend of Zelda Echoes of Wisdom finally lets players control Nintendo’s titular heroine. The game mixes the top down perspective of 2D Zelda games with gameplay from the series’ newer, open world 3D entries. Paper Mario The Thousand Year Door is generally regarded as the best Mario RPG, and the Switch remake is well worth revisiting.


Luigi’s Mansion 3 takes place in a haunted hotel overrun with ghosts, retaining the Luigi as a Ghostbuster gameplay the series has employed since 2001. Nintendo Switch Sports didn’t change the world the way Wii Sports did in 2006, but it remains a fun party game with six sports including tennis and bowling.

Splatoon 3 is the latest entry in Nintendo’s paint laden shooter franchise with a deeper single player mode. Kirby’s Return to Dream Land Deluxe is an HD remake of a Wii game that introduces additional abilities, mini games and an entirely new section available after completing the original story.

No Switch bundle deals this year

Typically, Nintendo offers a discount on a Switch bundle that includes a digital copy of a first party title, but that doesn’t seem to be happening this year. The company has openly admitted it’s shifting focus to the Switch 2 and recently raised the price of the original Switch due to tariff policy changes.

Sadly, no Switch 2 games are part of the promotion. However, shoppers can play the discounted original Switch titles on Nintendo’s latest console. For those looking for deals related to the Switch 2, some retailers are running video game promotions giving shoppers a chance to save on newer releases.

Amiibo also discounted

Beyond games, several amiibo figurines are seeing steep price cuts. Characters from The Legend of Zelda Tears of the Kingdom including Tulin, Yunobo, Riju and Sidon are down to $10 from $30, representing a 67% discount. These amiibo grant random items in both Tears of the Kingdom and Breath of the Wild.

Street Fighter 6 amiibo featuring characters like Jamie, Kimberly and Luke are down to $15 from $40. These figurines let players save outfit, color and custom button settings while also unlocking in game rewards including music and wallpaper.

The holiday sale runs through November 28th at Nintendo and select retailers, giving shoppers several days to take advantage of the discounts before they disappear.

data.title: Black Friday iPad Air deal shows why less costs more
data.description:
This year’s Black Friday shopping frenzy has taken an unexpected turn: Apple is steering bargain hunters away from its own retail channels and toward Amazon for some of the season’s most compelling discounts. Leading the charge is the iPad Air 11-inch with M3 chip, 128GB storage, and WiFi connectivity, now available for $449—a substantial $150 drop from its $599 list price and an unprecedented low for this configuration.

The discount raises an intriguing question for anyone considering a tablet purchase: Does Apple’s middle child offer the sweet spot between affordability and performance, or are consumers better served by the budget-friendly base iPad or the premium iPad Pro?


The Middle Ground That Actually Makes Sense

Apple’s iPad lineup has long presented a paradox. The entry-level iPad offers accessibility but limited power. The iPad Pro delivers cutting-edge technology at a premium that many users struggle to justify. The iPad Air, however, occupies a carefully calibrated middle position that suddenly feels less like a compromise and more like intelligent design.

This year’s model brings desktop-class computing power through its M3 chip—the same silicon architecture that drives MacBook Air laptops. That means genuine multitasking capability across resource-intensive applications, from graphics-heavy gaming to 4K video editing, all packaged in a device that weighs barely over a pound. The integration of Apple Intelligence throughout the operating system adds another dimension, embedding artificial intelligence features that enhance writing, organization, and task management without requiring separate applications or subscriptions.


The Liquid Retina display spans 11 inches with P3 wide color gamut support and True Tone technology, ensuring color accuracy whether you’re retouching photographs, streaming content, or reviewing design work. An ultralow reflectivity coating minimizes glare, making the device practical for outdoor use or brightly lit office environments. The 60Hz refresh rate proves adequate for most applications, though those accustomed to the 120Hz ProMotion technology found in iPad Pro models may notice the difference during rapid scrolling or stylus work.

Connectivity That Keeps Pace With Modern Demands

Wi-Fi 6E compatibility ensures the iPad Air connects seamlessly with the latest router technology, delivering faster speeds and improved performance in congested wireless environments. The 6GHz band substantially reduces interference, translating to more stable video call connections and accelerated file transfers. The USB-C port handles charging while also supporting data transfer and connections to external displays or storage devices.

The front-facing 12MP camera features Center Stage technology, which automatically adjusts framing during video calls to keep users centered as they move—a feature that has evolved from novelty to necessity in an era of remote work and virtual family gatherings. The rear 12MP camera captures high-quality photos and 4K video, proving surprisingly capable for document scanning and spontaneous content creation.

Battery performance remains impressive, with all-day endurance that eliminates the anxiety of outlet hunting during extended work sessions, flights, or marathon coffee shop occupations.

iPad Air: Strategic Positioning in Apple’s Ecosystem

The $449 Black Friday price point positions this iPad Air configuration in fascinating territory. The base iPad, priced at $349, runs on the older A14 chip and lacks Apple Intelligence integration. The iPad Pro, starting at $999, features OLED display technology and the newer M4 processor alongside professional-grade cameras that most consumers will never fully utilize.

The iPad Air splits the difference by delivering modern M3 performance and Apple Intelligence capabilities while foregoing the premium display technology and supplementary features that appeal primarily to creative professionals. For the vast majority of users—students, remote workers, content consumers, and casual creators—this represents optimal value.

Apple Pencil Pro compatibility adds another dimension for those interested in digital art, note-taking, or precise interface control. The stylus integration feels natural and responsive, though potential buyers should note that the Apple Pencil Pro represents an additional investment beyond the tablet itself.

The Amazon Factor

Apple‘s decision to route significant Black Friday discounts through Amazon rather than its own channels reflects broader shifts in retail strategy. While Apple Stores maintain their reputation for customer service and hands-on experience, Amazon’s logistics network and Prime membership ecosystem offer convenience that increasingly drives purchasing decisions.

The partnership also allows Apple to maintain brand positioning while still participating in Black Friday’s discount culture—a delicate balance for a company that traditionally resists heavy promotional activity.

Final Considerations

At $449, the iPad Air 11-inch with M3 chip represents exceptional value for anyone seeking more capability than entry-level tablets provide without paying for professional features they won’t use. The combination of genuine computing power, all-day battery life, quality display technology, and Apple Intelligence integration creates a compelling package.

This Black Friday deal transforms the iPad Air from a sensible compromise into an actively smart purchase. Whether you’re upgrading from an older tablet, seeking your first iPad, or looking for a portable computing solution that doesn’t sacrifice performance, this configuration deserves serious consideration before inventory depletes or pricing returns to normal.

The deal is available now through Amazon, though Black Friday demand typically means popular configurations sell out quickly.

data.title: This Gemini feature makes NotebookLM 10x more useful
data.description:
Google is taking steps to make its artificial intelligence ecosystem more cohesive by introducing a feature that connects Gemini with NotebookLM. The upcoming update will allow users to import notebooks directly from NotebookLM into the Gemini platform, creating a smoother experience for those who rely on both tools for their daily work.

Recent code updates reveal that Google plans to add a visible link to NotebookLM within the Gemini interface. This addition represents part of a larger initiative to eliminate friction points and help users move between the two platforms without interruption. The change signals Google’s recognition that many users depend on multiple AI tools and need them to work together harmoniously.


The integration comes as Google continues investing heavily in its AI portfolio, seeking to compete with rivals like Microsoft and OpenAI. By making its products work better together, the company hopes to create an ecosystem that keeps users engaged across multiple services rather than forcing them to choose between competing platforms.

Expanding capabilities through enhanced attachments

  1. The Attachments menu in Gemini is receiving a significant expansion that will change how users interact with the platform.
  2. Users currently can import code, upload photos and attach various file types through the existing menu system.
  3. The new notebook option will join these existing features, giving users another way to bring external information into their Gemini workspace.

This expanded menu demonstrates Google’s commitment to making Gemini a central hub for different types of content and data. Rather than forcing users to jump between applications or manually copy information from one place to another, the enhanced Attachments menu aims to bring everything together in a single location where it can be accessed and analyzed efficiently.


The notebook import feature specifically addresses a pain point for users who have invested time organizing information in NotebookLM. Instead of recreating that work or switching back and forth between applications, they will soon be able to pull their structured notebooks directly into Gemini conversations and workflows.

Transforming research and professional workflows

While Google has not released detailed information about exactly how the notebook import function will operate, the implications for certain user groups appear significant. Researchers who spend considerable time gathering and organizing information in NotebookLM will benefit from quick access to that material when working within Gemini.

Professionals who frequently transition between the two platforms should find their daily routines becoming more efficient. The ability to reference notebooks without leaving Gemini could eliminate numerous context switches throughout the workday, allowing for deeper focus and more productive sessions.

The feature also suggests Google understands how its users actually work with AI tools. Rather than treating each product as a standalone solution, the company recognizes that real-world workflows often involve multiple applications working in concert. By facilitating these connections, Google makes its tools more valuable to users who have already invested time learning both platforms.

Technical implementation still under development

The feature remains in development, with no confirmed release date announced by Google. This early stage means certain aspects of the implementation may change before the public launch. Users should expect refinements and adjustments as Google tests the integration and gathers feedback from early adopters.

Code updates indicate the groundwork is being laid for the connection between NotebookLM and Gemini, but the exact user experience remains to be seen. Questions about how notebooks will display within Gemini, whether users can edit imported content, and how updates sync between platforms have not yet been answered publicly.

Despite the uncertainty around specific details, the direction Google is taking seems clear. The company wants to create a unified experience across its AI products, making it easier for users to leverage the strengths of each tool without facing barriers when moving between them.

Building a competitive AI ecosystem

Google’s focus on connecting Gemini and NotebookLM reflects broader competition in the artificial intelligence space. As companies race to offer the most capable and user-friendly AI tools, integration and ecosystem strength have emerged as key differentiators alongside raw technical capabilities.

Microsoft has taken a similar approach with its Copilot integration across Office applications, demonstrating the value users place on tools that work seamlessly together. Google appears to be following this playbook, recognizing that individual product excellence matters less than overall ecosystem functionality for many users.

The notebook import feature also positions Google to serve knowledge workers who need sophisticated information management capabilities. By making it easier to organize, access and work with structured information across platforms, Google strengthens its appeal to professionals, academics and researchers who represent some of the most demanding AI users.

What users can expect moving forward

As Google continues refining this integration, users can anticipate additional enhancements that further connect the Gemini ecosystem. The company has shown commitment to iterative improvement, regularly rolling out features that respond to user needs and competitive pressures.

The notebook import capability represents just one piece of a larger strategy to make Google’s AI tools indispensable for users seeking flexible knowledge management solutions. Future updates may bring even tighter integration, potentially allowing real-time collaboration features or advanced analysis tools that leverage data from both platforms simultaneously.

For now, users interested in the upcoming feature will need to wait for Google to complete development and begin rolling out access. Those who have built extensive notebook collections in NotebookLM have the most to gain from this enhancement, as it promises to unlock new ways of working with their carefully organized information.

The update underscores Google’s understanding that artificial intelligence tools deliver the most value when they adapt to how people actually work rather than forcing users to adapt to rigid product boundaries.

data.title: How Apple M4 Max redefines laptop performance now
data.description:
Apple’s approach to chip design has undergone a fundamental transformation since the debut of the M1 processor in 2020. The M4 Max reveals how dramatically the company has shifted power allocation away from the CPU toward graphics processing and memory systems, redefining what performance means in modern MacBook Pros.

The power balance evolution

When Apple launched its first M1 processor in 2020, marking the transition away from Intel chips, the focus centered heavily on CPU efficiency. Early M1 designs dedicated roughly 18W to 25W of their total power budgets specifically to the CPU, with the remainder supporting graphics and memory bandwidth.


The M1 Max drew approximately 115W in total system power, but allocated only 25W to its CPU cores. This distribution established a pattern that would continue evolving through subsequent chip generations as Apple refined its silicon architecture and identified where additional power investment produced the greatest performance returns.

The power balance began shifting noticeably with the arrival of the M1 Pro and M1 Max in 2021. These chips allocated a far greater chunk of total power to the GPU rather than the CPU, signaling Apple’s recognition that many professional workflows depend more heavily on graphics processing than raw CPU speed.


M4 Max power distribution

The M4 Max, which arrived in 2024 and introduced Thunderbolt 5 support to Mac computers for the first time, demonstrates how far this evolution has progressed. The CPU is no longer the principal consumer of thermal headroom within Apple’s chip designs.

Current estimates suggest the M4 Max CPU draws approximately 48W within a roughly 70W total chip envelope. This means Apple allocates substantial power to graphics processing and memory bandwidth rather than pushing more watts toward the processor cores. The distribution reflects a fundamental reassessment of where power investment produces the most meaningful performance gains.

The newest M5 generation, which rolled out last week and powers the latest 14-inch MacBook Pro and iPad Pro, has reduced CPU power consumption even further. The processor draws a maximum of just 15W from a 25W total power budget, representing a dramatic decrease compared to earlier generations.

Future projections for M5 Max

While Apple hasn’t announced when an M5 Max might launch, historical patterns suggest a roughly six-month gap between base chip releases and their Pro and Max variants. The M5 Max will likely increase total chip power compared to the base M5, but without a proportionally large increase in CPU power consumption.

Data estimates generated by analyzing past Apple chip trends suggest the M5 Max CPU might use approximately 50W from a 95W overall design. This proportion remains nearly identical to the M4 Max’s distribution, indicating Apple believes it has found an optimal balance between processor power and other chip systems.

Efficiency plateau reached

Apple’s power scaling appears to have reached a point where the CPU cores have become efficient enough that adding additional wattage provides minimal performance benefits. The law of diminishing returns has set in for processor power consumption, making investments in other chip components more worthwhile.

Multi-core CPU benchmark scores have risen relatively modestly from the M1 Max’s 13,188 to approximately 25,000 in the M4 Max. While this represents meaningful improvement, the gains pale in comparison to advances in other areas of chip performance.

GPU performance explosion

GPU performance has climbed dramatically across the same timeframe. The M1 Max scored roughly 112,000 in graphics benchmarks, while the M4 Max achieves significantly higher scores. Projections for the M5 Max suggest graphics performance exceeding 200,000, representing nearly double the capability of the original M1 Max.

This massive improvement in graphics processing capability reflects where Apple has chosen to invest additional power budget and engineering resources. For many professional workflows involving video editing, 3D rendering and visual effects work, GPU performance matters far more than raw CPU speed.

Neural engine advancement

The neural engine has experienced perhaps the most dramatic evolution of any chip component. The first M1’s neural engine delivered 11 TOPS, which seemed impressive at the time. The new M5 has jumped to an estimated 133 TOPS, supporting Apple Intelligence features that run entirely on-device without cloud processing requirements.

Projections suggest the future M5 Max could reach approximately 400 TOPS of neural engine performance. This massive increase enables sophisticated machine learning tasks to run locally on MacBook Pros, opening new possibilities for AI-powered creative tools and productivity applications.

Redefining professional performance

Rather than chasing peak CPU output that might only matter in specific benchmark scenarios, Apple is optimizing for sustained mixed workloads that combine CPU, GPU and AI processing simultaneously. This approach reshapes what users should consider when evaluating professional MacBook capabilities.

For creative professionals working with video editing, 3D modeling, music production and machine learning tasks, the payoff comes from how efficiently the chip moves data between components and balances power across different processing systems. Single-threaded CPU speed matters less than the overall system’s ability to handle complex workflows that engage multiple chip components simultaneously.

Memory system importance

The memory system has become equally crucial to overall performance as the GPU. Apple’s unified memory architecture allows the CPU, GPU and neural engine to access the same pool of high-bandwidth memory without copying data between separate memory spaces.

This architectural advantage means memory bandwidth and capacity directly impact how well the entire system performs under real-world professional workloads. Apple’s decision to allocate significant power budget to maintaining high memory bandwidth reflects this reality.

The mature efficiency point

In Apple’s base M5 chip, the CPU has reached what engineers call a mature efficiency point. Additional power investment in processor cores produces diminishing returns compared to investing that same power budget in graphics processing, neural engine capability or memory bandwidth.

The true cost of performance in modern Apple silicon now lies with the GPU and memory system rather than the CPU. This represents a fundamental shift from traditional computer architecture, where processors consumed the lion’s share of system power and defined overall performance capabilities.

Apple’s evolving approach to chip design demonstrates how the company thinks about professional computing needs and where future performance gains will come from as silicon technology continues advancing.

data.title: Can ChatGPT now do group chats? OpenAI explains the feature
data.description:
OpenAI has officially launched group chat functionality for ChatGPT, marking a pivotal evolution in how users interact with artificial intelligence. The feature, which underwent brief testing in select regions earlier this month, is now available globally across all subscription tiers including Free, Go, Plus and Pro plans. The rollout allows up to 20 people to collaborate simultaneously with the AI, transforming the chatbot from a personal assistant into a shared workspace.

The timing of this release aligns with growing demand for collaborative digital tools, positioning ChatGPT as more than just a question-and-answer service. OpenAI has designed the feature for diverse applications, from coordinating travel plans to developing collaborative outlines or even serving as an impartial referee to settle friendly debates.


Getting started with group conversations

Setting up a group chat requires minimal effort. Users simply tap the people icon located in the corner of any existing chat window, and ChatGPT generates a new group thread while keeping the original conversation private. This design choice ensures that personal exchanges remain separate from collaborative sessions.

Participants join through shareable links, similar to popular messaging platforms. When joining for the first time, users create a display name, username and profile photo to help others identify them within the group. This personalization layer adds a human element to what might otherwise feel like an impersonal AI interaction.


The technical backbone of group chats relies on GPT-5.1 Auto, an intelligent system that selects the most appropriate model for each response based on the subscription levels of participants. This approach ensures quality remains consistent regardless of who has which plan tier, while efficiently managing computational resources.

ChatGPT develops social intelligence

One of the most compelling aspects of this launch involves OpenAI teaching ChatGPT contextually appropriate social behaviors. The AI now understands when to contribute to conversations and when to remain silent, mimicking the natural rhythm of human group discussions. It can deploy emoji reactions and reference user profile photos when generating personalized images, adding layers of informal communication that reflect how people actually interact online.

All standard ChatGPT capabilities remain functional in group settings. Participants can use web search, upload files, generate images and utilize voice dictation. Notably, rate limits only apply when ChatGPT itself responds, not to human messages, preventing any single user from consuming the group’s allocated interactions. Users can always force a response by tagging ChatGPT directly.

Privacy and control take center stage

OpenAI has positioned privacy as a cornerstone of the group chat experience. The feature operates independently from ChatGPT’s personal memory system, meaning the AI won’t reference individual conversation histories during group discussions. Conversely, group interactions don’t create new memories that might surface later in private chats.

Users maintain full control over their experience. Notifications can be muted, ChatGPT’s behavior can be customized for each specific group, and participants can leave at any time without hassle. The company has also implemented automatic content filtering for users under 18, applying stricter moderation standards to protect younger participants.

What this means for AI collaboration

OpenAI frames group chats as the first step toward richer shared experiences with artificial intelligence. By positioning ChatGPT as an active participant rather than merely a tool, the company hints at future developments that could further integrate AI into collaborative workflows.

The practical applications appear limitless. Teams can brainstorm ideas together with AI assistance, friends can plan events with an extra set of digital hands, and students can work through problems collectively. The feature bridges the gap between personal productivity and social interaction, suggesting that AI’s role in daily life will increasingly involve group dynamics rather than isolated exchanges.

As conversational AI becomes embedded in mainstream workflows, group chat functionality may prove essential for broader adoption. Whether users embrace ChatGPT as a collaborative partner or find the concept awkward remains to be seen. What’s clear is that OpenAI has committed to exploring how AI fits into collective rather than individual contexts, potentially reshaping expectations about what chatbots can accomplish.

data.title: Tesla driver dies after fiery crash in Arizona suburb
data.description:
Fatal collision raises renewed safety concerns

A Tesla driver lost their life Tuesday evening after the electric vehicle crashed at an intersection in Buckeye, Arizona and erupted in flames, according to Fox10 Phoenix. The tragic incident occurred shortly after 5 p.m. near the edge of a residential subdivision, adding another troubling entry to the electric car manufacturer’s mounting safety concerns.

The driver, who was alone in the vehicle, died at the scene. Emergency responders faced significant challenges extinguishing the blaze, which stemmed from a damaged lithium battery experiencing thermal runaway. This type of fire is notoriously difficult to control with conventional firefighting methods, requiring a specialized hazmat crew to fully suppress the flames.


Officials have not yet released details about the Tesla model involved or the circumstances leading to the crash. Critical questions remain unanswered, including whether driver error contributed to the collision or if the company’s self-driving technology played any role in the fatal accident.

Company faces mounting legal challenges

The Buckeye crash comes as Tesla grapples with numerous lawsuits and federal scrutiny over vehicle safety. In August, the company settled a $329 million lawsuit related to a 2019 collision that killed a 22-year-old woman and seriously injured her boyfriend. Investigators determined that Tesla’s Autopilot system bore partial responsibility for that deadly crash.


Just one month later in September, Tesla reached settlements in two additional California lawsuits where the Autopilot feature was implicated in separate fatal incidents, both occurring in 2019. These legal battles highlight ongoing concerns about the reliability and safety of the company’s autonomous driving technology.

Federal investigation targets door design flaws

Tesla now faces federal investigation over a potentially dangerous door design flaw that could trap occupants during emergencies. The system has been reported to malfunction or become difficult to operate when drivers need to escape quickly from their vehicles.

The door defect has spawned multiple lawsuits against the automaker. Most recently, a new lawsuit was filed in Washington state this week concerning a January 2023 crash that resulted in one woman’s death and left her husband suffering severe burns on his legs. The legal action alleges the faulty door design prevented or delayed their escape from the burning vehicle.

These cases underscore a disturbing pattern where Tesla’s design choices may be putting drivers and passengers at increased risk during accidents and fires.

Robotaxi program struggles with safety issues

Tesla’s challenges extend beyond its consumer vehicles to its commercial autonomous driving ventures. The company’s Robotaxi service, launched in Austin and San Francisco in June, has experienced a troubled rollout marked by frequent accidents and operational mishaps.

The self-driving taxis have been involved in numerous crashes since their introduction earlier this year. In one particularly concerning incident, a Tesla safety supervisor was discovered sleeping behind the wheel of a Robotaxi while it navigated San Francisco streets, raising serious questions about the program’s oversight and safety protocols.

Questions about company priorities

As Tesla confronts these escalating safety issues and legal battles, some observers are questioning whether company leadership is adequately focused on addressing the problems. The mounting safety concerns and continued accidents suggest the electric vehicle manufacturer may need to dedicate more resources and attention to resolving these critical issues before they claim additional lives.

For now, the investigation into Tuesday’s fatal crash in Buckeye continues as authorities work to determine exactly what caused the collision and subsequent fire that took one person’s life. The incident serves as a sobering reminder of the potential dangers associated with electric vehicle battery fires and the ongoing challenges facing Tesla’s safety record.

data.title: Bloodborne anniversary treat sells out in just 1 hour
data.description:
A decade of hunting nightmares

Bloodborne reached its 10th anniversary in 2025, marking a full decade since FromSoftware’s gothic masterpiece first terrified and delighted PlayStation 4 players on March 24, 2015. While Sony remained conspicuously silent when the actual anniversary date passed in March, the company has finally acknowledged the milestone with an official celebration that takes an unexpected form.

Rather than the remaster or remake many fans have been requesting for years, Sony partnered with Laced Records to create something different entirely. The result is a meticulously crafted vinyl soundtrack collection that honors one of gaming’s most haunting and beautiful musical scores.


Three vinyl options hit the market

The anniversary celebration centers on two distinct volumes of Bloodborne music now available for purchase. Bloodborne Vol. 2 features nine tracks from The Old Hunters downloadable content, the expansion that added new areas, bosses and storylines to the original game. This volume can be purchased separately for fans who specifically want the DLC soundtrack.

Bloodborne Vol. 1 represents a Laced Records exclusive re-release of the soundtrack originally published in 2016. This collection includes 21 songs from the base game, capturing the atmospheric tension and epic boss battle themes that defined the Bloodborne experience for millions of players.


The collector’s dream package

The premium offering combines both volumes into a three-LP collector’s boxset that includes additional exclusive items. The package features a gorgeous collector’s box and slipmat designed specifically for this release. At $98, the complete set offers surprisingly reasonable pricing for collectors accustomed to premium video game merchandise commanding much higher prices.

The relatively accessible price point makes this release attainable for dedicated fans without requiring the kind of financial commitment that limited editions sometimes demand. However, that affordability combined with limited quantities means these sets likely won’t remain available for long.

Musical excellence from multiple composers

The Bloodborne soundtrack represents a collaborative effort from six talented composers who each brought unique perspectives to the game’s audio landscape. Ryan Amon, Yuka Kitamura, Tsukasa Saitoh, Nobuyoshi Suzuki, Cris Velasco and Michael Wandmacher worked together to create the distinctive sound that perfectly complements the game’s Victorian horror atmosphere.

Their work ranges from haunting ambient pieces that underscore exploration through Yharnam’s twisted streets to bombastic orchestral arrangements that accompany battles against nightmarish creatures. The soundtrack stands as one of gaming’s most accomplished musical achievements, deserving recognition beyond just accompanying gameplay.

Fans express mixed emotions

The announcement generated immediate excitement across gaming communities, though reactions reveal some complicated feelings about what this release represents. Reddit discussions show fans genuinely appreciating the quality and thoughtfulness of the vinyl collection while simultaneously expressing frustration about what it isn’t.

One enthusiastic fan celebrated the surprise announcement, noting that Sony did ultimately deliver something special for the 10th anniversary. They praised the gorgeous presentation and attention to detail evident in the product images and descriptions.

Others struck a more bittersweet tone. One commenter observed that Sony seems willing to produce every conceivable type of Bloodborne merchandise except the one thing fans most desperately want, whether that’s a full remaster, remake or even just a simple 60fps performance patch for modern PlayStation consoles.

Quality validated by previous releases

Skeptical collectors worried about audio quality received reassurance from owners of Laced Records’ previous Bloodborne vinyl release. One fan who purchased the 2019 Moonlight Great Sword edition, which featured green vinyl, vouched enthusiastically for the production values. They emphasized that the quality justified the price and warned that the original pressing sold out within an hour of becoming available.

That sellout warning carries particular weight for this new release. Limited edition gaming collectibles frequently disappear quickly, and vinyl records face additional constraints due to manufacturing limitations. Interested buyers face pressure to make purchase decisions rapidly or risk missing out entirely.

The remaster question looms large

While vinyl enthusiasts celebrate this release, it inevitably highlights the absence of technical improvements to the game itself. Bloodborne remains locked at 30 frames per second on PlayStation hardware, despite the PlayStation 5’s capabilities that could easily handle enhanced performance. The game has never received official support for higher frame rates or visual improvements that have benefited other Sony exclusives from the same era.

This technical stagnation frustrates fans who want to experience Bloodborne with modern performance standards. The existence of fan-made patches that unlock 60fps performance on modified hardware only emphasizes that the technical barriers don’t exist. Sony simply hasn’t prioritized creating an official solution.

Why Sony’s silence continues

The company’s reluctance to greenlight a Bloodborne remaster or remake puzzles industry observers. The game maintains a passionate fanbase that regularly expresses willingness to pay for enhanced versions. Similar titles from the same period have received remasters, remakes or at minimum performance patches for current generation hardware.

Various theories attempt to explain Sony’s position. Some speculate about complicated licensing arrangements with FromSoftware or the game’s director. Others suggest Sony views the property as less commercially viable than sales figures and fan engagement would indicate. The truth remains unclear, leaving fans to speculate while hoping each major gaming announcement might finally deliver news about Bloodborne’s future.

Looking toward future anniversaries

One Reddit user jokingly suggested that perhaps the 15th anniversary in 2030 will bring the technical improvements fans desire. While meant humorously, the comment reflects genuine uncertainty about whether Bloodborne will ever receive the modern treatment its reputation deserves.

The vinyl release demonstrates Sony hasn’t forgotten about Bloodborne entirely. The company recognizes the game’s cultural significance and dedicated fanbase enough to commission high-quality physical products celebrating its music. Whether that recognition will eventually translate into interactive experiences beyond the original PlayStation 4 version remains an open question.

For now, fans who appreciate premium physical media can celebrate this tangible acknowledgment of Bloodborne’s 10th anniversary, even as they continue hoping for the digital improvements that remain frustratingly out of reach.

data.title: Google unveils powerful Nano Banana Pro AI image tool
data.description:
Google DeepMind introduced Nano Banana Pro on Nov. 20, marking a significant evolution in artificial intelligence-powered image generation technology. The new model, built on Gemini 3 Pro, represents the company’s latest effort to provide both casual creators and professionals with tools that transform ideas into polished visual content. The release comes just months after Google launched the original Nano Banana model, which focused primarily on basic image editing tasks like photo restoration and creating miniature figurines.

Nano Banana Pro distinguishes itself through enhanced reasoning capabilities that leverage Gemini’s real-world knowledge to produce more contextually accurate visuals. The system can generate educational infographics, convert handwritten notes into structured diagrams and create data visualizations that communicate complex information through imagery. This advancement moves beyond simple aesthetic improvements, aiming instead to make AI-generated images more useful and informative for practical applications.


Text rendering reaches new heights

One of the model’s most notable improvements addresses a persistent challenge in AI image generation: creating legible, accurately rendered text within images. Nano Banana Pro excels at incorporating text directly into visuals, whether users need short taglines or lengthy paragraphs. The system handles multiple languages and can localize content for international audiences, making it easier for creators to scale their work across different markets or share materials with family and friends who speak different languages.

The enhanced text capabilities open new possibilities for designers working on mockups, posters and branded materials. Users can experiment with various textures, fonts and calligraphy styles while maintaining readability. The model understands depth and nuance in ways that previous versions could not, allowing for more sophisticated integration of typography into complex visual compositions. This functionality proves particularly valuable for creating storyboards, architectural renderings and marketing materials that require precise text placement.


Professional-grade creative controls

Nano Banana Pro introduces advanced editing features that give users granular control over their creations. The system allows creators to select, refine and transform specific portions of images through improved localized editing tools. Users can adjust camera angles, modify focus points and apply sophisticated color grading techniques that previously required professional software and expertise. The model even enables dramatic lighting transformations, such as converting daytime scenes to nighttime or creating specific photographic effects like bokeh.

The consistency features represent another major advancement, allowing users to blend up to 14 images while maintaining the resemblance of up to five people across complex compositions. This capability helps designers bridge the gap between initial concepts and finished products, whether transforming sketches into realistic renderings or ensuring brand consistency across multiple touchpoints. Creators can apply desired visual styles to mockups while preserving the identity of people and objects throughout multi-element scenes.

Real-world knowledge integration

By connecting to Google Search’s knowledge base, Nano Banana Pro can incorporate real-time information into generated images. The system can create recipe visualizations using current culinary data, generate weather infographics based on live meteorological information or produce sports-related graphics reflecting up-to-date statistics. This integration of factual accuracy with creative image generation helps ensure that educational materials and informational graphics contain correct, current details rather than plausible-looking but potentially inaccurate content.

The model’s enhanced reasoning allows it to understand context better than previous versions, producing images that reflect not just visual aesthetics but also logical relationships between elements. When creating diagrams or infographics, Nano Banana Pro considers how information flows and connects, resulting in more intuitive and educational visual explanations.

Availability and access tiers

Google is rolling out Nano Banana Pro across multiple products and services with different access levels. Free-tier users of the Gemini app receive limited quotas before reverting to the original Nano Banana model, while Google AI Plus, Pro and Ultra subscribers enjoy higher usage limits. The technology is being integrated into Google Ads for advertisers, Google Workspace tools including Slides and Vids, and developer platforms like the Gemini API and Google AI Studio.

All images generated by Google’s AI tools include SynthID, an imperceptible digital watermark that helps identify AI-generated content. Users can now upload images to the Gemini app and ask whether Google AI created them, with the system detecting the embedded watermark. Free and Pro tier users will see a visible Gemini sparkle watermark on their creations, while Ultra subscribers and developers receive images without visible marks for professional work requiring clean visual canvases.

Source: Google Keyword blog
